rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO JACOBO VS. AMERICAN SECURITY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 1, 2011, Line 1. OTHER NAVIGATORS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. To the extent that moving party is making the motion on its own behalf, it is not a party to the case. To the extent that moving party is making the motion on behalf of the Estate of Scott Peters, moving party lacks standing to bring the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 1, 2011, Line 8. DEFENDANT WADE AUBRY,M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue. Plaintiff¿s claims are barred by the one-year statute of limitations of CCP § 340.5. The letter to Dr. Aubry dated October 7, 2009 constitutes a notice of intent to sue under § 364 and thus the second notice dated May 5, 2010 is ineffective to toll the limitations period. (See Bennett v. Shahhal (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 384, 389-90). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 1, 2011, Line 12. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to all causes of action challenged by the demurrer except the second cause of action which is stricken and the fifth cause of action which is sustained with five days leave to amend. Declaratory relief is a proper cause of action. The alleged tender offered per Civil Code § 1500 is not a bar to plaintiff¿s claims. The second cause of action is stricken, sua sponte, because plaintiff did not have leave to add this cause of action. Plaintiff may move for leave to amend his complaint. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend, per plaintiff's request. Plaintiff is granted leave to re-plead his breach of contract cause of action in a second amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR MARTINEZ et al VS. INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 1, 2011, Line 14. DEFENDANT INTUITIVE SURGICAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first and seventh causes of action, sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth, sixth and eighth causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action. The learned intermediary doctrine raises factual issues not amenable to resolution on demurrer. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of the implied warranty is sustained with leave to amend so plaintiff can allege privity between the parties more clearly. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for breach of the express warranty is sustained without leave to amend as unopposed. The sixth and eight cause of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation are sustained with leave to amend to plead them with more particularity. The demurrer to the seventh cause of action for concealment is overruled. That claim requires less particularity and plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendant owed a duty to disclose because of defendant¿s exclusive knowledge and/or partial suppression. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

App. 4th 97, 112-18; Southern Pacific Land Co. v. Westlake Farms, Inc. (1987) 188 Cal. App. 3d 807, 816-17). The third claim for promissory estoppel is adequately pled. rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO CAMBARERI et al VS. ASH K. GUJRAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 1, 2011, Line 18. DEFENDANTS TRUE COMPASS LLC AND MIGSIF, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. On September 26, 2011 Judge Woolard denied defendants¿ ex parte application for relief from the timing requirements of CCP 425.16(f). Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, lacks the authority to reach a different result. However, even if Judge Kahn had authority to grant relief from the timing requirements, defendant has not shown good cause for such relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
